meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Build Faster with AI
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of SaaS knowledge with this professional starter kit. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps in record time.
content.answer_block: Stop reinventing the wheel. The solution to limited SaaS knowledge is a pre-configured SaaS starter kit that automates authentication, database schema, and payment integration. By pairing Antigravity’s boilerplate architecture with Claude Code’s autonomous engineering, you transform from a struggling freelancer into a high-velocity product builder capable of shipping production-ready SaaS in days.
content.intro:
The modern freelance market is brutal: clients demand enterprise-grade SaaS platforms, but the learning curve for backend architecture, auth flow, and database scaling is a wall most developers hit at full speed. You spend weeks configuring boilerplate instead of shipping features, watching your margins evaporate while the competition iterates in real-time.

But the game has fundamentally changed. You no longer need to be a systems architect to build a robust SaaS. By leveraging a high-performance starter kit alongside intelligent agentic workflows, you can bypass the 'no-knowledge' trap entirely. We are moving from manual coding to orchestration, allowing you to deliver professional-grade software that scales while you focus on the UI and business logic.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a production-ready codebase using the Antigravity framework. This eliminates the need to manually configure environment variables or database connections.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS starter project with Next.js, PostgreSQL, and Clerk authentication using the Antigravity core architecture.

steps.title: Configure Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models using natural language. Let the AI handle the complex migrations and relationship mapping.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including User, Organization, Subscription, and Project entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate prisma schema with relational connections for multi-tenancy'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Don't write payment webhooks from scratch. Use the starter kit's built-in Stripe integration to handle subscription tiers automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks and checkout sessions for a three-tier subscription model within the existing Antigravity auth flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'integrate stripe billing webhooks and secure subscription routes'

steps.title: Build the Client Dashboard
steps.description: Rapidly prototype the user dashboard using pre-built components that match your design system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard layout with sidebar navigation, user settings, and project management stats using Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement responsive dashboard layout using existing ui library'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Configure CI/CD to push code to production automatically, ensuring your client sees progress every time you commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Vercel deployment configuration with automated preview environments for every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure automated ci/cd pipeline for production readiness'

pro_tips: Always initialize your database with 'soft deletes' enabled to avoid permanent data loss during client testing phases.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth middleware first; if your security layer is solid, the rest of the app can scale safely.
Rename your environment variables early to match your specific cloud provider to prevent deployment-time failures.
Keep your 'Prompt Library' in a local file so you can reuse the same architecture patterns across all your freelance projects.
Use the 'diff' command in Claude Code to review every architectural change before committing to ensure no boilerplate bloat is introduced.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, focus on creating a 'client-handover' document that explains how to manage their new SaaS, and then use your newfound speed to scale your freelance business by taking on two additional clients simultaneously.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start mastering the architecture. With these tools, you aren't just a coder; you're a SaaS factory. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
